perturbation.original_text: 9. Compensation (a) In consideration of the rights granted by Individual hereunder, Lifeway agrees to pay Individual a royalty (the "Royalty") equal to $0.02 for each Lifeway product or individual item sold by Lifeway during each calendar month of the Term bearing Individual's first name, last name or other identifying personal characteristics; provided, however, the Royalty will cease being paid upon the death of Individual. Notwithstanding anything to the contrary herein, the Royalty will not exceed $50,000 in any month. All undisputed Royalty payments shall be made in U.S. dollars in cash or to the order of Individual and shall be due and payable within thirty (30) days after the end of each calendar month for sales during the previous month.
perturbation.changed_text: 9. Compensation (a) In consideration of the rights granted by Individual hereunder, Lifeway agrees to pay Individual a royalty (the "Royalty").  All undisputed Royalty payments shall be made in U.S. dollars in cash or to the order of Individual and shall be due and payable within thirty (30) days after the end of each calendar month for sales during the previous month.
perturbation.explanation: The clause specifying the Royalty amount ($0.02 per item) and the maximum monthly Royalty ($50,000) has been removed. This introduces an in-text contradiction because while the section mentions royalty payments, the actual amount to be paid is undefined. This lack of definition contradicts the intent of a compensation clause, making the agreement ambiguous and potentially unenforceable. This change will cause uncertainty and potential disagreement about what the Individual is actually owed. Furthermore section 1 states the licensee has a "royalty-free" right to use the individual's likeness.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 9. Compensation (a) In consideration of the rights granted by Individual hereunder, Lifeway agrees to pay Individual a royalty (the "Royalty").  All undisputed Royalty payments shall be made in U.S. dollars in cash or to the order of Individual and shall be due and payable within thirty (30) days after the end of each calendar month for sales during the previous month.
perturbation.justification: The changed text omits the specific royalty amount ($0.02 per item) and the maximum monthly royalty ($50,000) that were present in the original text. This creates a direct contradiction within section 9, as it mentions 'Royalty' without defining its value. The contradicted text in section 9 also mentions a royalty payment without any specific value, causing the contradiction to be more pronounced. Without a defined amount, the obligation to pay a 'Royalty' is ambiguous and unenforceable, leading to an in-text contradiction based on the definition of Omissions - In Text Contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: 1. License - Individual grants Lifeway together with its affiliates, subsidiaries, parent companies and their representatives and employees have an unlimited, perpetual, non-exclusive, worldwide and, except as set forth in Section 9, royalty-free, right to use, reuse, publish, reproduce, perform, copy, create derivative works, exhibit, broadcast, and display throughout the world the name, image and likeness of Individual in Marketing Materials (as defined below) in connection with marketing, advertising or otherwise promoting the Lifeway products and/or services and for historical reference and display purposes and other internal purposes, including without limitation, internal sales meetings. Notwithstanding anything herein to the contrary, Lifeway shall be under no obligation to cause the Marketing Materials to be displayed, published or performed. Individual further waives the right to inspect or approve the Marketing Materials.
perturbation.changed_text: 1. License - Individual grants Lifeway together with its affiliates, subsidiaries, parent companies and their representatives and employees have an unlimited, perpetual, non-exclusive, worldwide right to use, reuse, publish, reproduce, perform, copy, create derivative works, exhibit, broadcast, and display throughout the world the name, image and likeness of Individual in Marketing Materials (as defined below) in connection with marketing, advertising or otherwise promoting the Lifeway products and/or services and for historical reference and display purposes and other internal purposes, including without limitation, internal sales meetings. Notwithstanding anything herein to the contrary, Lifeway shall be under no obligation to cause the Marketing Materials to be displayed, published or performed. Individual further waives the right to inspect or approve the Marketing Materials.
perturbation.explanation: The phrase 'except as set forth in Section 9, royalty-free' has been removed from the licensing clause. This creates a contradiction with Section 9 (Compensation), which explicitly outlines a royalty payment structure. By removing this exception, the licensing grant appears to be entirely royalty-free, conflicting with the payment obligations established in Section 9.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 9. Compensation (a) In consideration of the rights granted by Individual hereunder, Lifeway agrees to pay Individual a royalty (the "Royalty").  All undisputed Royalty payments shall be made in U.S. dollars in cash or to the order of Individual and shall be due and payable within thirty (30) days after the end of each calendar month for sales during the previous month.
perturbation.justification: The original text explicitly states the license is 'except as set forth in Section 9, royalty-free.' The changed text omits this exception, making the license appear royalty-free without any conditions. This directly contradicts Section 9, which details the payment of royalties by Lifeway to the Individual. The omission creates a clear and direct conflict between the two sections regarding whether royalties are due, and if so, how they are calculated and paid.

perturbation.original_text: 6. Non-Competing Services - Individual agrees that, during the Term hereof, she will not render similar services for, or permit the use of her name, nickname, likeness, voice, live or recorded performance, photograph, signature or facsimile thereof, and biographical materials in advertising or publicizing in any medium for any other Kefir product, yogurt product, cheese, frozen desserts and other products that compete with products manufactured or distributed by Lifeway and its affiliates, subsidiaries and parent companies other than those products manufactured or distributed by Lifeway and its affiliates, subsidiaries, and parent companies.
perturbation.changed_text: 6. Non-Competing Services - Individual agrees that she will not render similar services for, or permit the use of her name, nickname, likeness, voice, live or recorded performance, photograph, signature or facsimile thereof, and biographical materials in advertising or publicizing in any medium for any other product other than those products manufactured or distributed by Lifeway and its affiliates, subsidiaries, and parent companies.
perturbation.explanation: By omitting "Kefir product, yogurt product, cheese, frozen desserts and other products that compete with products manufactured or distributed by Lifeway and its affiliates, subsidiaries and parent companies" This creates uncertainty around the individual agreement to not permit the use of her name for competing products, specifically Kefir, which may be seen as a contradiction because the contract is between the individual and Lifeway, which sells Kefir.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 6. Non-Competing Services - Individual agrees that she will not render similar services for, or permit the use of her name, nickname, likeness, voice, live or recorded performance, photograph, signature or facsimile thereof, and biographical materials in advertising or publicizing in any medium for any other product other than those products manufactured or distributed by Lifeway and its affiliates, subsidiaries, and parent companies.
perturbation.justification: The changed text omits specific products (Kefir, yogurt, cheese, frozen desserts) from the non-compete clause. This creates a contradiction because the contract is with Lifeway, a company that manufactures Kefir. By removing this specific category, it implies that the individual *can* render services for competing Kefir products, directly conflicting with the implied purpose of the original non-compete agreement as it pertains to Lifeway's core product. The explanation states this, which is the reason for the 'YES' designation.
